reviews.review_text:
The last true battlefield that had almost everything a true bf fan wants these days. 

• I remember in 2013 playing first time and it felt like no first person shooter will ever be close to Battlefield 4 back then, and guess what, its 2026 and still no shooter feels the same like Battlefield 4.

• It was ahead of its time, a unique in its genre and not only, the current devs will never make us another game like this even if battlefield 6 isn't that bad in some way.

• This game has lived through many generations of people, we were young and in school still when this game released, many people already grew up and moved on from gaming, some decided to stay on battlefield 4 to this day, just like me.

• Battlefield 4 has still a decent amount of people playing this game to this day, a big online, full servers even if not all the time and not everyday, it still offers us the fun we once had

• This game's servers even been DDoSED for like 1 or 2 years by weirdos that wanted to leave this game dead and they failed, people started to host better servers so they face it well, and the online has been better lately,

• Battlefield 4 truly remains the most toxic battlefield ever, even the silly bunny hop that sweats do on operation locker 24/7 is pure nostalgic to see these days, Can't even be mad at them anymore.

• My favourite battlefields ever were Battlefield 2 (2005) and a newer representation of Battlefield 2 on New Frostbite engine, the Battlefield 3, which is a legendary entry in the franchise, well Battlefield 4 felt like these two games mixed all in for me and it delivered what i missed in battlefield 3 from BF2. For example the chinese maps, the chinese army itself.

• Battlefield 4 has all of it, and i am grateful we got to see a real Battlefield 2 successor, even if some things changed, devs moved on from some stuff, it doesnt matter, it gave me really the same vibes, hell, even the Dragon Valley map was in Battlefield 4 which was my childhood map id play for hours.

• This game had a rough launch, but eventually they fixed and turned into something people will enjoy after years and years. A truly gem. 

Still remains the most unbeatable battlefield game as for now.

10/10 game.

Would play another 10 years without thinking that id get too old for it.

https://youtu.be/_33nzqwm_o0?si=riyXJZz1cvtN3g-e

Battlefield 4 is still awesome. The level of detail, the destruction, the graphics, and most importantly—the storyline is simply fantastic, just like in every Battlefield game.
But the bots are just awful. They spawn right under your nose or even behind you, and your allies don’t do a thing. They might just stand there in front of enemies and not fire a single shot. In reality, you’re the only one actually fighting the whole battle.
Overall, I recommend playing through it, but be prepared - the NPCs are absolute hell.

Im coming back after years of not playing to see i need a seperate EA account to play and it also need BF4 purchased im not rebuying a game i bought years ago because some money grubbers. Hope EA changes this but i doubt it

I had more sympathy for the bugged civilian who clips through your van in the Shanghai mission than for any of the 'main characters'. literally nothing but the first mission was well made, because it was the mission they used to market for the game's graphics. 6/10 and that's because I loved the multiplayer.

Even on Easy difficulty, the campaign is still frustrating to get through. The AI teammates are completely useless. I'll ping enemies right in front of them, and they just stand there doing absolutely nothing instead of helping.

The FOV is also terrible. I set it to the maximum value and it still feels like I'm playing at 50 FOV.

The game can be fun at times, but the campaign is a mess. I honestly wouldn't recommend buying it.

so optimized and impressive combat, guns recoils are awesome. thumbs up for the game.
Great game awesome graphics great missions thanks EA.
If you are into FPS Games that are set back in the golden oldie days then BF 4 is a bathtub of nostalgia.  I am still trying to work out which is better of the two battlefield 4, or battlefield 6.

Still one of the best Battlefields ever made. Can still get into a populated match easy, though you need to use the Battlelog server browser to see accurate player numbers (don't need to be logged in to use Battlelog, just to edit your profile/emblem/etc, you can even check your stats, unlocks, rewards, assignments, etc. without logging in by typing in the URL directly, e.g. (...)/bf4/user/<your name here>/, pro tip). It's a benchmark for extremely tight projectile gunplay with a lot of attention to detail and excellent sound design. Weapon customisation is also great, though weapons and attachments can be fairly grindy to unlock since it's based on getting kills and it's a strictly linear progression. Vehicles are powerful but not unstoppable; skillful play is required to do well with them. Just a really well-tuned multiplayer experience overall.

The campaign is pretty terrible, honestly. I'm not sure why they bothered. There are some weapons you can unlock by playing it but last I heard, some update actually broke it so you wouldn't get the unlocks, and it'd been that way for a while. No idea if that's still the case but basically, don't be surprised if it's still broken. The guns aren't anything too special, at least, and there's PLENTY to choose from.

In my opinion, every Battlefield that came after this was worse in some way. It also regularly goes on sale for really, really cheap. Highly recommended.

BF4 is a giant… Levolution… Over BF3 on almost all fronts. Contains a much better campaign, with characters, development, and even relationships. Sadly, the story is even more marred by yet another pro-interventionist plot, where once again the game portrays the USA as "good" and Russia and China as "bad" without any real developments this time, just black and white.

The gameplay is still perfect, even better now, with even more weapons. Strangely, I found the sound design to be a downgrade, it wasn't as intense or nuanced as BF3.

The graphics, on the other hand, are a massive leap. So many elements on the screen are moving and interacting. Wind, leaves, flying paper, and smoke, but the star of the show is the destruction, which has been significantly scaled with their "levolution" technology. The levels are very fun to play, longer, more dynamic, and more open than BF3 but not as big or destructible as BFBC2 though.

Also, it runs perfectly, yet again. And no more "browser tab" shenanigan, sadly we still have to open EA app…

As for the multiplayer, it's great. I have a few hours in PS4 and in Origin, it's quite possibly the best BF multiplayer of them all, so yeah, definitely recommend it.
Clunky interface and linear play.  This game feels dated and I found it annoying to play.
this game has brought me nothing but joy and happiness from the people i have met and the sweats i have killed along the way

This is literally my favorite game of all time. From the ps4 in 2016 to now I have never felt any ill will for this game i love it so much

Battlefield 4 is probably the best Battlefield their is in my opinion. Originally played it on Xbox 1 when it first came out. Recently re-bought it on steam and enjoying playing against fellow players who played the game when I did back in the day. I would recommend this game still to everyone.

going on 13 Years old and still better than BF6... may not be the most beautiful game in todays standard but gameplay wise its still miles over anything nowadays. no live service models, pure content with peoples hearts and souls poured into it. no insane attachment locks and no unsightly skins that make you wonder what someone was smoking to make it. Bugs? yea but not more than most modern live service games. metas? kinda but realistically whatever you like the feel of will work.

multiplayer is great fun, some of the out of bounds mechanics can be irritating though (when you bail out of a plane/ helicopter you can be killed by the out of bounds). game feels like a nice balance of realism and arcade, though it leans more toward arcade.

Prepare for a bunch of god awful, inexcusable bugs when trying to play the campaign. The glare is awful, you can't adjust the single player fov without third party tools, and the swimming bug is the ABSOLUTE WORST

Mislim da je bila 1982, još dok sam bio student, tamo u doba Sovjetskog Saveza. Ja kupio fotoaparat. Znaš u to vreme nije bilo ove moderne tehnologije, ovih telefona i andraka..

Znači ti lepo staviš film, namotaš i škljoc, pa opet namotaš i škljoc. To su bili ti ♥♥♥♥♥ fotoaparati, oni dobri. Bila ona olimpijada u Moskvi, pa je aparat imao kao tih 5 krugova i pisalo "Moskva 1980.", a ja bio 82. i ostalo mi još par komada, tako da znam da je bila 1982.

Ne samo to, nego mi u moskvi dobili hotel de bi prenoćili,a to ustvari bio čitav kompleks za sportiste koji su učestvovali na olimpijadi. To bilo tu negde izmedju 25 i 30 kilometara do Crvenog trga i tu bio onaj mauzolej Lenina. I sad mi idemo tog jednog dana, idemo gledati Lenina. Lepo mi došli autobusom i to u onu veliku gužvu, mi stanemo u red da cekamo i vidimo ono kilometar ispred nas ulaz, par hiljada ljudi se lepo poredjalo i neki prodavac išao redom i nudio ljudima kobasice. 
I mi polako dolazimo na red, gde kaze onaj čuvar meni: "ne može se u kratkim pantalonama ući u mauzolej, Džabe vam je da stojite sad 2 sata". A JA OTIŠO SAMO DA BIH VIDEO LENINA ČOVEČE. i ja odem do autobusa i pitam vozača: "Dobar dan majstore, hajd da zamenimo gaće, razumeš, da ja mogu Leninu da se poklonim", i on me gleda jako zbunjeno i kaže: "ma ne dolazi u obzir", te ja reko JAOOOO i tu meni suze naoči idu i tako to... Te kaže on: "Odvešću ja tebe do hotela da se presvučeš". ZNAČI, čovek je vozio prazan autobus, samo nas dvojicu, nazad 30 kilometara kako bih ja obukao dugačke pantalone i vratio me 30 kilometara nazad do onog Crvenog trga, i to sve jer ako njega zateknu na radnom mestu u kratkimm pantalonama, kaki čoveče, Sibir odma...

 I što je najveći fazon, kad smo mi stigli tamo, oni naši taman prošli.. Tako da nisam video Lenina u životu, eto, živim promašen život, eto, skroz

Unfortunately, there are a lot of bugs that makes your path way more harder (especially that one with pushing you back in water). Enemies can spawn out of nowhere and go to nowhere, I had an episode where they were coming through a wall. In additional, they can shoot you when they out of your sight. Its far from the worst game out there and is still pretty damn fun to play all things considered, but the pains of a 13 year old game that was undercooked when it released and was left rare as hell by its devs by the end of its life cycle are starting be felt. Its still playable, but it is showing its age rather agressively these days. With netcode jumbled together like a ransom note, bugs/janky ass physics that only continue getting worse as hardwear improves, power tripping admins on every server and a campaign that barely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ functions anymore. 

I wish Battlefield 4 was as fun and enjoyable as I remember playing as a kid but time has not been as kind as I would've liked to this monumental entry into the series, as is the same with Battlefield 3.

Overall it is a very decent game. 
The best parts is the graphics, voiceacting, level designs and the plot. What held me inside this game to the end of the story was that while playing you see the amount of work creators put into the game, after you finish two chapters and get used to all the buttons and weapons everything becomes real and you are experiencing everything that the main characters go through. 
The chinese and russian NPC enemies do not sound scripted, when they run they scream something to each other wich makes the experience more real. 
Everything that happens to you is scripted but somehow, of the amount of variations of weapon you choose, how your enemies proceed to move towards you, this game feels very realistic, when the tanks shoot at you feel how vulnurable you are as a man, you feel the heavy blast falling beside you, you hear the parts of the metal hiting you and you recieve damage - this kind of details makes this experience of war feel real.

What was hard and createt a tiny feeling of bitternes is when you play the Compaign on a Hard mode you will suffer: your allies are not making damage - you are the only one taking out all enemies, sometimes the enemies could stand face to face with you allies and nobody will get killed, they just scream and shoot untill you kill the enemies. 
The hardest missions was those few times you were forced to use heavy machinery against tanks, (got to replay that mission like 15 times before I finally got through) and when enemies used RPG's and Grenades, you feel like a hunted rabbit, don't know where the bomb came from and get significant damage.

Battlefield 4 is still a fun military shooter that delivers plenty of explosive action, memorable set pieces, and large-scale battles. Even today, the graphics hold up surprisingly well for a game released over a decade ago. Character models, environments, and effects still look good enough to remind players why Battlefield 4 was considered a visual showcase in its time.

Unfortunately, this is also a game that has not aged particularly well on PC. The optimization is poor compared to modern standards, and I honestly do not remember the console version being this janky when I originally played it. One of my biggest frustrations is the requirement to use the EA launcher. There are times when the game simply refuses to launch, and troubleshooting launcher issues becomes part of the experience.

Once in the game, the problems continue. The campaign is plagued with moments where controls suddenly become unresponsive or feel inconsistent. There are sections where aiming and targeting enemies feels off, resulting in missed shots that should have connected. These issues can pull you out of the action and make certain encounters more frustrating than challenging.

That said, the game itself remains entertaining. The story is serviceable, the action sequences are exciting, and there is still plenty of Battlefield-style chaos to enjoy. If you've never played Battlefield 4 before, I can still recommend experiencing the campaign at least once.

However, if you have access to a console version, that is the version I would suggest playing. The PC version's launcher dependency and technical issues make it difficult to recommend unless EA eventually improves support or removes the requirement to use their launcher. Battlefield 4 remains a good game underneath the technical problems, but those problems are hard to ignore in 2026.

Rating: 6.5/10

Battlefield 4:

Is still one of my favourite multiplayer FPS games, even after all these years.

I started with Battlefield 3 on PS3, then played Battlefield 4, and later got it again on PC. That probably says enough about how much I enjoy it. The multiplayer is the main reason to play this game. Large maps, vehicles, infantry combat, destruction, squads, and those chaotic Battlefield moments all come together really well. When everything clicks, Battlefield 4 is still one of the best multiplayer shooters out there.

The gameplay loop is fun, addictive, and easy to keep coming back to. You can play infantry, tanks, helicopters, boats, support roles, objective modes, or just focus on unlocking weapons and attachments. The Levolution events and destructible environments also help matches feel more dynamic, especially on maps like Siege of Shanghai.

The campaign is decent and has some good moments, but Battlefield 4 is clearly at its best in multiplayer. The game is getting old now, so that is the main thing holding it back. Some parts feel dated, and depending on servers and region, your experience may vary.

The achievement hunt is a mix of fun, easy, and grindy. If you already enjoy the multiplayer, it is not too bad, but some parts will take time.

Overall, I would still recommend Battlefield 4, especially on sale due to its age. If you are an FPS player, Battlefield fan, casual player, or achievement hunter, this is still worth playing.

Battlefield 4 — More of Battlefield 3, for Better and Worse

Battlefield 4 doesn’t really feel that different from the legendary Battlefield 3. At its core, it’s mostly the same formula — just with new maps, more weapons, updated mechanics, and the addition of China as a new major faction.

The multiplayer is still chaotic and cinematic in the best Battlefield style, full of destruction and those classic “only in Battlefield” moments. But compared to BF3, the game feels more like an expansion than a true evolution.

Where the game really falls short is the campaign. The story feels much weaker this time. In short, brave American soldiers fight Russians for unclear reasons, then suddenly get involved in a political coup in China, which eventually leads to war with China — and sometimes Russia again. The plot feels messy and far less memorable than Battlefield 3.

Sadly, the multiplayer today feels mostly dead, which is disappointing for a game that was built around large-scale online battles. Finding active servers with a good connection and normal settings is becoming harder every year.

Still, despite its flaws, Battlefield 4 remains an enjoyable military shooter and a nostalgic reminder of the golden era of Battlefield.

The game is full of bugs

Even after all these years, Battlefield 4 remains the undisputed king of large-scale multiplayer shooters, and looking at its consistently positive reception, the community clearly agrees. In an era where modern FPS titles are heavily plagued by bloated launchers, restrictive skill-based matchmaking, and severe content droughts, returning to the raw, unadulterated chaos of BF4 feels like a much-needed breath of fresh air. Playing this masterpiece on a modern high-end rig is an absolute revelation; being able to max out every graphical setting and witness the massive 'Levolution' destruction, the incredibly crisp audio design, and the flawless gunplay at incredibly high frame rates is pure gaming bliss. The core mechanics here are exactly what made the franchise legendary. The traditional class system heavily rewards actual squad coordination, the vehicle combat possesses a genuine learning curve, and the weapon customization is remarkably deep without feeling like a tedious chore. Every single match consistently delivers those unscripted, cinematic moments that newer titles desperately try and fail to replicate, whether you are pushing through the absolute meat grinder of Operation Locker or parachuting off a collapsing skyscraper in the middle of Siege of Shanghai. The fact that community servers are still heavily populated and thriving speaks volumes about its enduring mechanical quality. It is a brilliant, timeless military sandbox that absolutely embarrasses most modern AAA shooters and remains a mandatory experience for any serious fan of the genre

When its fun, its fun il give you that. But its hard to even get in the mood for this game. While I understand the appeal for some people the guns feel too clunky and slow, while the TTK is too fast.

Honestly if you can get it for like 3 dollars, sure. But its not really worth it otherwise.

There are players here that have played since 2013 and are pro at the game. If it's not the helicopters destroying a team it's the player with 60 kills and 3 deaths that will make you rage. I used to play on 360 and xbox one back in the day and those lobbies are no where near as sweaty as on pc.

This game is so peak but the Campaign is so glitchy in the first mission u need to shoot a helicopter with a grenade launcher but when u shoot the helicopter the grenade goes through the helicopter but atleast multiplayer is peak

The campaign has some good moments but the AI is horrible. The other characters aren't able to kill enemies, even when you use the "engage" mechanic. Also all the enemies only attack you. So you're literally by yourself when the game isn't designed that way, making it way harder than it's intended to be. If that's not bad enough, half the combat sequences are very far apart and require snipers. Except the snipers usually don't even work. Also there's a horrible water glitch in the third mission. Also the valkarie dissapeared at one point and when I reloaded, it saved over it being gone. So I needed to replay the whole mission all over again from the beginning. Not to mention there are trees in the campaign that bend hilariously every time you or anyone else touch them. And it's especially stupid because it happens 100% of the time because a character takes cover behind it right in front of you when you spawn in. So 100% of players will notice it but  they vcouldn't be bothered to fix it.And the final two missions are garbage. They're literally just you infinitely shooting people with broken physics and AI, no set peices or anything interesting. On the finally mission I just got sick of it and looked it up on youtube so I could just see what happened.
Everyone goes crazy about the online mode too but it's really nothing special either. The levolution stuff looks cool but most of it doesn't really change the map or anything. And most lobbies turn it off anyway. The vehicles are kinda fun ig. I'm just glad I got it on sale for $2.
I bought every single game because they were all on sale, and everyone says this one is the best. If that's the case, I am deeply disappointed. It's a broken campaign and a surprisingly forgettable multiplayer.

10/10

Battlefield 4 still stands as one of the best large-scale FPS games. The multiplayer delivers chaotic, cinematic battles with great map design, destruction, and a strong mix of infantry and vehicle combat. The class system rewards teamwork, and even today it’s easy to find active servers.

However, it’s not flawless. The campaign is forgettable, and some technical issues and server quirks still show up. New players might also struggle against longtime veterans.

Pros

* Massive, chaotic multiplayer battles
* Great destruction and map variety
* Fun vehicles and teamwork focus

Cons

* Weak campaign
* Occasional bugs and server issues
* Hard for beginners

Final Score: 8.5 / 10
